title: physaria carinata subsp. carinata
intro: Physaria carinata subsp. carinata, also known as the prairie bladderpod, is a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and potentially treating conditions such as arthritis and other inflammatory diseases. The roots and leaves of the plant can be used to make herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
